deployment_execution_phase.overview: This phase handles the final deployment of the REST connector(s) after all DSL configuration files have been created. It provides four deployment options: Local (Docker), Serverless, Self-hosted Server, and OpenShift.

phase_4_multiple_connectors.title: Multiple Connectors Check
phase_4_multiple_connectors.description: After completing Phase 3 (Presentation & Confirmation) for a connector, ask if the user wants to create additional connectors.

step_1_ask_additional_connectors.title: Ask About Additional Connectors
step_1_ask_additional_connectors.when: After successfully creating and confirming a connector configuration
step_1_ask_additional_connectors.question: Would you like to create another REST API connector configuration?

option.name: yes
option.label: Yes, create another connector
option.action: Return to Phase 1 (API Discovery & Analysis) to create another connector
option.note: All connectors will be stored in the same resources directory

step_1_ask_additional_connectors.presentation_format: Present options clearly: 1. Yes - Create another connector (returns to Phase 1) 2. No - Proceed to deployment

loop_behavior.description: If user chooses to create another connector, repeat Phases 1-3 for the new connector. Continue asking after each connector until user chooses to proceed to deployment.
loop_behavior.note: Multiple connectors can be packaged together in a single deployment. All DSL files in sdk-gen/subprojects/connectors_forge_rest/src/main/resources/ will be included in the deployment.

phase_5_deployment_option_selection.title: Deployment Option Selection
phase_5_deployment_option_selection.description: Present four deployment options and guide the user through the selected deployment process.

step_1_present_deployment_options.title: Present Deployment Options
step_1_present_deployment_options.question: How would you like to deploy your REST API connector(s)?
step_1_present_deployment_options.important_note: The deployment options presented depend on the deployment type chosen in Phase 0: - Developer option: Shows all 4 deployment options (Local, Serverless, Self-Hosted, OpenShift) - Codeless option: Shows only 3 deployment options (Serverless, Self-Hosted, OpenShift) Local deployment is not available for codeless option as it requires building custom Docker image.

options_for_developer.title: Options when Developer deployment type was chosen in Phase 0

option.name: local
option.label: Local Docker Deployment
option.description: Build and run Docker container on your local machine. Best for: Testing and manual verification of connectors.

option.name: serverless
option.label: Serverless Deployment
option.description: Deploy Docker container to a serverless platform. Best for: As-a-service solutions with automatic scaling.

option.name: self_hosted
option.label: Self-Hosted Server Deployment
option.description: Deploy Docker container to your own server infrastructure. Best for: As-a-service solutions with full control.

option.name: openshift
option.label: OpenShift Deployment
option.description: Deploy container to OpenShift platform. Best for: On-premises enterprise deployments.

options_for_codeless.title: Options when Codeless deployment type was chosen in Phase 0
options_for_codeless.note: Local Docker deployment is not available - codeless uses pre-built image (marek02/connectors-forge:latest)

option.name: serverless
option.label: Serverless Deployment
option.description: Deploy pre-built Docker container to a serverless platform. Best for: As-a-service solutions with automatic scaling.

option.name: self_hosted
option.label: Self-Hosted Server Deployment
option.description: Deploy pre-built Docker container to your own server infrastructure. Best for: As-a-service solutions with full control.

option.name: openshift
option.label: OpenShift Deployment
option.description: Deploy pre-built container to OpenShift platform. Best for: On-premises enterprise deployments.

step_1_present_deployment_options.presentation_format: - For Developer option: Present all four deployment options with clear numbering - For Codeless option: Present only three deployment options (exclude Local Docker) - Renumber options for codeless (1-3 instead of 2-4) - Indicate which options are placeholders (not yet implemented) - Explain that codeless uses pre-built image marek02/connectors-forge:latest

sub_step_1_ask_execution_preference.question: Would you like me to execute the deployment commands, or would you prefer to do it yourself?

option.name: auto
option.label: Automatic - Execute commands for me
option.description: I will run all necessary Gradle tasks to build and start the Docker container

option.name: manual
option.label: Manual - I'll do it myself
option.description: I will provide you with step-by-step instructions

action.name: Navigate to sdk-gen
action.note: All Gradle tasks must be run from the sdk-gen directory

action.name: Build Project
action.description: Compiles the project and runs tests
action.note: This may take several minutes on first run

action.name: Generate Flight Application
action.description: Generates the Flight service application with all connectors

action.name: Build Docker Image
action.description: Creates Docker image containing the connector(s)
action.note: Requires Docker to be installed and running

action.name: Start Docker Container
action.description: Starts the Docker container with the connector(s)
action.note: Container will run in the background

action.name: Verify Deployment
action.description: Confirm that the container is running successfully. The connector(s) should now be accessible via the Flight service.

action.name: Inform User
action.message:
Local deployment complete!
Your REST API connector(s) are now running in a Docker container. The Flight service is accessible and ready to use.
To stop the container, run: ./gradlew dockerStop (from sdk-gen directory)

if_build_fails.action: Report the error details to the user
Suggest checking build logs for specific issues
Offer to provide manual instructions instead

if_docker_not_running.action: Inform user that Docker must be installed and running

sub_step_2b_manual_deployment.title: Manual Local Deployment Instructions
sub_step_2b_manual_deployment.instructions:
To deploy your REST API connector(s) locally using Docker, follow these steps:
1. Navigate to the sdk-gen directory: cd sdk-gen
2. Build the project: ./gradlew build
This compiles the project and runs tests. May take several minutes on first run.
3. Generate the Flight application: ./gradlew generateFlightApp
This generates the Flight service application with all your connectors.
4. Build the Docker image: ./gradlew dockerBuild
This creates a Docker image containing your connector(s). Note: Docker must be installed and running on your machine.
5. Start the Docker container: ./gradlew dockerStart
This starts the container with your connector(s) running.
6. Verify the deployment: The connector(s) should now be accessible via the Flight service.
To stop the container later, run: ./gradlew dockerStop
Let me know once you've completed these steps or if you encounter any issues.

wait_for_confirmation.description: Wait for user to confirm they have completed the deployment steps or report any issues encountered.

placeholder_notice.message:
Serverless deployment is not yet implemented.
This is a placeholder for future functionality. When implemented, this option will: - Build the Docker image with your connector(s) - Deploy the image to a serverless platform (e.g., AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Run, Azure Functions) - Configure automatic scaling and endpoint management - Provide the service URL for accessing your connector(s)
For now, please choose one of the other deployment options: - Option 1: Local Docker Deployment (for testing) - Option 3: Self-Hosted Server Deployment (for production)

future_implementation.description: When implemented, this section will include: - Cloud provider selection - Authentication and credentials configuration - Serverless platform-specific deployment commands - Endpoint URL generation and configuration - Monitoring and logging setup

step_4_handle_self_hosted_deployment.title: Self-Hosted Server Deployment
step_4_handle_self_hosted_deployment.important_note: The deployment process differs based on the deployment type chosen in Phase 0: - Codeless: Uses deploy-container.sh script with pre-built image (marek02/connectors-forge:latest) - Developer: Uses ./gradlew dockerPush to build and push custom Docker image

sub_step_1_ask_execution_preference.question: Would you like me to execute the deployment, or would you prefer to do it yourself?

option.name: auto
option.label: Automatic - Execute for me
option.description: I will run the deployment commands/script to deploy to your server

option.name: manual
option.label: Manual - I'll do it myself
option.description: I will provide you with instructions

sub_step_2a_codeless_automatic.title: Automatic Self-Hosted Deployment (Codeless)
sub_step_2a_codeless_automatic.when: User chooses automatic execution AND codeless deployment type

action.name: Execute Deployment Script
action.description: Runs the OS-specific deployment script that handles: - Using pre-built Docker image (marek02/connectors-forge:latest) - Deploying to the target server - Configuring networking and access
action.note: The script selection is based on the operating system: - Unix/Linux/macOS: deploy-container.sh (requires execute permissions: chmod +x) - Windows: deploy-container.bat

action.name: Monitor Deployment
action.description: Monitor the script output for successful deployment confirmation.

action.name: Verify Deployment
action.description: Confirm that the connector(s) are accessible on the target server.

action.name: Inform User
action.message:
Self-hosted deployment complete!
Your REST API connector(s) have been deployed to your server using the pre-built image. The deployment script has configured the necessary networking and access.
Check the script output for the service endpoint URL.

if_script_fails.action: Report the error details from script output
Check if server credentials are configured correctly
Verify network connectivity to target server
Offer to provide manual instructions instead

sub_step_2b_developer_automatic.title: Automatic Self-Hosted Deployment (Developer)
sub_step_2b_developer_automatic.when: User chooses automatic execution AND developer deployment type

action.name: Navigate to sdk-gen
action.note: Gradle task must be run from sdk-gen directory

action.name: Push Docker Image
action.description: Builds the Docker image with your custom connector(s) and pushes it to the configured container registry. This task handles: - Building the Docker image with your connector code - Tagging the image appropriately - Pushing to your container registry
action.note: Container registry must be configured in gradle.properties or environment variables

action.name: Verify Push
action.description: Confirm that the image was successfully pushed to the container registry.

action.name: Inform User
action.message:
Docker image push complete!
Your custom Docker image with REST API connector(s) has been pushed to the container registry.
Next steps: - Deploy the image to your server using your deployment tools - Configure the service endpoint and networking - Verify the connector(s) are accessible

if_gradle_fails.action: Report the error details from Gradle output
Check if container registry credentials are configured
Check network connectivity to container registry
Offer to provide manual instructions instead

sub_step_2c_codeless_manual.title: Manual Self-Hosted Deployment Instructions (Codeless)
sub_step_2c_codeless_manual.when: User chooses manual execution AND codeless deployment type
sub_step_2c_codeless_manual.instructions:
To deploy your REST API connector(s) to your self-hosted server using the pre-built image:
For Unix/Linux/macOS: 1. Ensure the deployment script has execute permissions: chmod +x connectors-forge/codeless/deploy-container.sh
2. Run the deployment script: connectors-forge/codeless/deploy-container.sh
For Windows: 1. Run the deployment batch script: connectors-forge\codeless\deploy-container.bat
The script will: - Use the pre-built Docker image (marek02/connectors-forge:latest) - Deploy the container to your target server - Configure networking and access
Prerequisites: - Docker installed on your machine - Access credentials configured for your target server - Network connectivity to the target server
The script will output the service endpoint URL upon successful deployment.
Let me know once you've completed the deployment or if you encounter any issues.

wait_for_confirmation.description: Wait for user to confirm they have completed the deployment or report any issues encountered.

sub_step_2d_developer_manual.title: Manual Self-Hosted Deployment Instructions (Developer)
sub_step_2d_developer_manual.when: User chooses manual execution AND developer deployment type
sub_step_2d_developer_manual.instructions:
To deploy your custom REST API connector(s) to your self-hosted server:
1. Navigate to the sdk-gen directory: cd sdk-gen
2. Build and push your Docker image to the container registry: ./gradlew dockerPush
This will: - Build a Docker image with your custom connector code - Tag the image appropriately - Push it to your configured container registry
Prerequisites: - Docker installed and running on your machine - Container registry credentials configured in gradle.properties or environment variables - Network connectivity to the container registry
3. After the image is pushed, deploy it to your server using your deployment tools
4. Configure the service endpoint and networking on your server
5. Verify the connector(s) are accessible
Let me know once you've completed the deployment or if you encounter any issues.

wait_for_confirmation.description: Wait for user to confirm they have completed the deployment or report any issues encountered.

placeholder_notice.message:
OpenShift deployment is not yet implemented.
This is a placeholder for future functionality. When implemented, this option will: - Build the Docker image with your connector(s) - Create OpenShift deployment configurations - Deploy to your OpenShift cluster - Configure routes and services - Set up monitoring and logging - Provide the route URL for accessing your connector(s)
For now, please choose one of the other deployment options: - Option 1: Local Docker Deployment (for testing) - Option 3: Self-Hosted Server Deployment (for production)

future_implementation.description: When implemented, this section will include: - OpenShift cluster connection configuration - Project/namespace selection - BuildConfig and DeploymentConfig creation - Service and Route configuration - ImageStream setup - Resource limits and scaling configuration - Health checks and readiness probes

phase_6_cp4d_registration.title: Cloud Pak for Data Registration
phase_6_cp4d_registration.description: After successful deployment, register the connector(s) with Cloud Pak for Data. The registration process differs based on the deployment type chosen in Phase 0.

step_1_ask_registration_preference.title: Ask Registration Preference
step_1_ask_registration_preference.question: Would you like to register your connector(s) with Cloud Pak for Data now?

option.name: yes
option.label: Yes, register now
option.action: Proceed to registration process based on deployment type

option.name: no
option.label: No, I'll register later
option.action: Skip registration and complete the workflow

step_1_ask_registration_preference.presentation_format: Present options clearly: 1. Yes - Register connector(s) with CP4D now 2. No - Skip registration (can be done later)

step_2_developer_registration.title: Developer Option Registration
step_2_developer_registration.when: User chooses registration AND developer deployment type was selected in Phase 0

sub_step_1_verify_properties_file.title: Verify Properties File Creation
sub_step_1_verify_properties_file.important_note: Before registration, you need to create a properties file with your CP4D environment configuration. This file is not visible to me (it's in .bobignore), so you need to create it manually.

step.name: Create Properties File
step.description:
Create a properties file in the following location: sdk-gen/subprojects/flight/src/dist/resources/payload/envs/
Use a descriptive name like: myenv.properties, production.properties, or dev.properties
Base your file on the template at: sdk-gen/subprojects/flight/src/dist/resources/payload/envs/template.properties

step.name: Configure Required Properties
step.description: Your properties file must include: - apikey: Your IBM Cloud API key - auth_uri: Authentication endpoint (default: https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token) - datasource_types_uri: Datasource types API endpoint - flight_uri: URI of your deployed Flight service (grpc://host:port) - ssl_certificate_path: Path to your Flight service certificate (optional) - ssl_certificate_validation: Set to true/false

sub_step_1_verify_properties_file.confirmation_question: Have you created the properties file with your CP4D environment configuration? Please provide the name of your properties file (without .properties extension).

sub_step_2_ask_execution_preference.title: Ask Execution Preference
sub_step_2_ask_execution_preference.when: After user confirms properties file is created
sub_step_2_ask_execution_preference.question: Would you like me to execute the registration, or would you prefer to do it yourself?

option.name: auto
option.label: Automatic - Execute registration for me
option.description: I will run the Gradle register task with your environment configuration

option.name: manual
option.label: Manual - I'll do it myself
option.description: I will provide you with instructions to run the registration

action.name: Navigate to sdk-gen
action.note: Gradle task must be run from sdk-gen directory

action.name: Run Registration Task
action.description: Registers the connector(s) with Cloud Pak for Data using the specified environment configuration. Replace {env_name} with the properties file name provided by the user.
action.note: The env_name should match the properties file name without .properties extension

action.name: Verify Registration
action.description: Confirm that the registration completed successfully by checking the task output.

action.name: Inform User
action.message:
Registration complete!
Your REST API connector(s) have been registered with Cloud Pak for Data. The connector(s) are now available for use in your CP4D environment.
You can now create connections and access your REST API data through CP4D.

if_registration_fails.action: Report the error details from Gradle output
Verify the properties file exists and contains correct values
Check network connectivity to CP4D endpoints
Verify API key has proper permissions
Offer to provide manual instructions instead

sub_step_3b_manual_registration.title: Manual Registration Instructions (Developer)
sub_step_3b_manual_registration.instructions:
To register your REST API connector(s) with Cloud Pak for Data:
1. Ensure you have created your properties file in: sdk-gen/subprojects/flight/src/dist/resources/payload/envs/{your_env_name}.properties
2. Navigate to the sdk-gen directory: cd sdk-gen
3. Run the registration task with your environment name: ./gradlew register -Penv={your_env_name}
Replace {your_env_name} with your properties file name (without .properties extension) For example, if your file is myenv.properties, use: -Penv=myenv
The registration task will: - Authenticate with IBM Cloud using your API key - Register the connector datasource type with CP4D - Configure the Flight service endpoint - Make the connector(s) available in your CP4D environment
Prerequisites: - Valid IBM Cloud API key with proper permissions - Network connectivity to CP4D endpoints - Deployed and accessible Flight service
Let me know once you've completed the registration or if you encounter any issues.

wait_for_confirmation.description: Wait for user to confirm they have completed the registration or report any issues encountered.

step_3_codeless_registration.title: Codeless Option Registration
step_3_codeless_registration.when: User chooses registration AND codeless deployment type was selected in Phase 0

sub_step_1_verify_properties_file.title: Verify Properties File Configuration
sub_step_1_verify_properties_file.important_note: Before registration, you need to configure the properties file with your CP4D environment settings. This file is not visible to me (it's in .bobignore), so you need to modify it manually.

step.name: Locate Properties File
step.description: The properties file is located at: connectors-forge/codeless/register-envs.properties

step.name: Configure Required Properties
step.description: Edit the file and configure the following properties: - apikey: Your IBM Cloud API key - auth_uri: Authentication endpoint (default: https://iam.cloud.ibm.com/identity/token) - datasource_types_uri: Datasource types API endpoint - flight_uri: URI of your deployed Flight service (grpc://host:port) - ssl_certificate_path: Path to your Flight service certificate (optional) - ssl_certificate_validation: Set to true/false

sub_step_1_verify_properties_file.confirmation_question: Have you configured the properties file at connectors-forge/codeless/register-envs.properties with your CP4D environment settings?

sub_step_2_ask_execution_preference.title: Ask Execution Preference
sub_step_2_ask_execution_preference.when: After user confirms properties file is configured
sub_step_2_ask_execution_preference.question: Would you like me to execute the registration, or would you prefer to do it yourself?

option.name: auto
option.label: Automatic - Execute registration for me
option.description: I will run the registration script with your configuration

option.name: manual
option.label: Manual - I'll do it myself
option.description: I will provide you with instructions to run the script

action.name: Execute Registration Script
action.description: Runs the OS-specific registration script that handles: - Reading configuration from register-envs.properties - Authenticating with IBM Cloud - Registering the connector with CP4D - Configuring the Flight service endpoint
action.note: The script selection is based on the operating system: - Unix/Linux/macOS: register-connector.sh (requires execute permissions: chmod +x) - Windows: register-connector.bat

action.name: Monitor Registration
action.description: Monitor the script output for successful registration confirmation.

action.name: Verify Registration
action.description: Confirm that the connector(s) are registered and available in CP4D.

action.name: Inform User
action.message:
Registration complete!
Your REST API connector(s) have been registered with Cloud Pak for Data. The connector(s) are now available for use in your CP4D environment.
You can now create connections and access your REST API data through CP4D.

if_script_fails.action: Report the error details from script output
Verify the properties file is configured correctly
Check network connectivity to CP4D endpoints
Verify API key has proper permissions
Offer to provide manual instructions instead

sub_step_3b_manual_registration.title: Manual Registration Instructions (Codeless)
sub_step_3b_manual_registration.instructions:
To register your REST API connector(s) with Cloud Pak for Data:
1. Ensure you have configured the properties file at: connectors-forge/codeless/register-envs.properties
For Unix/Linux/macOS: 2. Ensure the script has execute permissions: chmod +x connectors-forge/codeless/register-connector.sh
3. Run the registration script: connectors-forge/codeless/register-connector.sh
For Windows: 2. Run the registration batch script: connectors-forge\codeless\register-connector.bat
The registration script will: - Read your configuration from register-envs.properties - Authenticate with IBM Cloud using your API key - Register the connector datasource type with CP4D - Configure the Flight service endpoint - Make the connector(s) available in your CP4D environment
Prerequisites: - Valid IBM Cloud API key with proper permissions - Network connectivity to CP4D endpoints - Deployed and accessible Flight service - Properly configured register-envs.properties file
Let me know once you've completed the registration or if you encounter any issues.

wait_for_confirmation.description: Wait for user to confirm they have completed the registration or report any issues encountered.

completion.title: Workflow Complete
completion.description: After successful deployment and optional registration, provide summary and next steps.

include.item: How to access and test the connector(s)

next_steps.step: Test the connector(s) by making API calls through the Flight service
If registered with CP4D: Create connections in CP4D UI
Configure client applications to use the connector endpoint

behavioral_rules.rule: Always ask about additional connectors after completing each configuration
Present all four deployment options clearly, indicating placeholders
For implemented options (Local, Self-Hosted), offer automatic or manual execution
For placeholder options (Serverless, OpenShift), explain future functionality
Execute Gradle tasks sequentially, waiting for each to complete
All Gradle tasks must be run from the sdk-gen directory
Provide clear error messages and recovery options

important_notes.note: Multiple connectors can be created and deployed together in a single container. All DSL files in the resources directory will be included.
Local deployment requires Docker to be installed and running on the user's machine.
Self-hosted deployment requires proper server credentials and network access.
Serverless and OpenShift deployments are placeholders for future implementation.
The deployment script (deploy-container.sh) must have execute permissions.
